Telecommunications

CIAM | Customer Data

The role:
Bridge the gap between the broad CIAM program and specialist projects involving identity verification, authentication, and customer data. You will lead a data-led approach to process improvement, developing reporting and controls to monitor the customer identity lifecycle.

What you'll be doing:

Map business requirements and lead the delivery of key priority initiatives, including end-to-end reporting for identity and authentication processes.

Maintain a CIAM risk register to ensure identity risk and compliance issues remain visible to senior leadership.

Proficiency in MS Project, MS Visio, and MS Excel, with strong analytical and problem-solving skills

Job Title: Senior Software Engineer- ML & Data Pipelines
Ad Text:
Senior Software Engineer – ML & Data Pipelines

We are a growing fintech scale-up, building the next generation of data-driven financial applications. We are looking for an experienced software and data engineer to help design, develop, and scale our systems and ML pipelines.
Responsibilities

Develop, maintain, and scale software systems and data pipelines that power analytics and ML applications.
Build, deploy, and monitor ML pipelines, ensuring reliability and performance in production.
Design and implement APIs and services to integrate ML models and data workflows into production systems.
Contribute to system and data architecture, offering practical, scalable solutions for complex problems.
Apply testing frameworks and best practices to ensure robust, high-quality software.
Work closely with team members to share knowledge, provide feedback, and drive continuous improvement.
Requirements

Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
5+ years of software development experience.
Strong experience with Python, SQL, and Linux.
Experience designing reliable, high-throughput batch or streaming systems.
Experience developing and integrating RESTful or gRPC APIs.
Practical experience with ML Ops – deploying, monitoring, and maintaining ML pipelines.
Familiarity with real-time NoSQL databases (e.g., Firestore, Bigtable, DynamoDB) is a plus.
Experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Cloud Build, Jenkins).
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to tackle complex technical challenges.
Why this role is exciting

Join a fast-growing fintech scale-up building innovative, high-impact systems.
Collaborate with a skilled, multidisciplinary team of engineers and data specialists.
Opportunity to grow your skills across software development, data engineering, and ML Ops.

ABOUT THE COMPANY
Our client is an industry leading brand who dominate their place in the Australian market. Currently looking to scale out their team with a brand new role. 

DUTIES
As a Lead Customer Analyst the core responsibilities of your role will cover:

Ensuring the teams and leaders you support have the insights and visibility they need to understand and explain key marketing, campaign optimization, audience, behavioural and competitor insights
Understanding customer behaviour and competitive pressures to identify opportunities to improve marketing effectiveness and growth
Applying statistical, analytical and data synthesis skills to understand the impact of marketing investments on business performance
Leading projects with marketing teams in their interpretation of campaign results, brand and performance health metrics, channel efficiency and the ROI of acquisition, retention and win back strategies
SKILLS
You will come with strong experience working in a complex consumer data driven environment, where managing and manipulating complex SQL data sets to uncover strategic insights has been a key focus. You will be able to demonstrate:  

Strong technical skills using SQL, manipulate and merge large volumes of data.
Being able to derive insights and present back to the business
Dashboarding skills with Tableau, or Looker 
At least 5 yrs experience in Data Analytics
Strong understanding of Statistics and Probability
Strong experience in customer analytics or marketplace environments
ML experience is a big advantage
Python essential
BENEFITS
This is an exciting opportunity to join an industry leading company while they continue to scale and grow with exciting new projects. 


HOW TO APPLY
Please click on the 'Apply' button below or email your resume to kate@correlateresources.com

Who we are

At Police Bank, we exist to serve those who serve. For over 60 years, we’ve supported our members with trusted, straightforward banking. We’re evolving into a more modern, member-first organisation one that combines strong foundations with forward-thinking ideas, where people are empowered to make an impact and do meaningful work.

The Opportunity

Based in the Projects team and reporting through to the Senior Delivery Lead, the Technical Business Analyst role is a business facing role that works with stakeholders to understand their processes, document needs and requirements into agreed artefacts, then works with project and stakeholder teams to design, implement and test agreed technical and process changes.

What you’ll be doing

Discovering, eliciting, refining and modelling business requirements for agreed  projects

Documenting processes, requirements and changes to agreed standard and format

Current state and future state process mapping

Identifying delivery and operational risks

Impact assessment and input to process and system solution design

Organising and coordinating business analysis efforts

Assessing the performance and value of solutions in use.

Previous experience as a Technical Business Analyst/Business Analyst within the  Banking or Financial Services space is a must.

Tertiary qualifications in Information Technology, Computer Science, Business, or a  related field (or equivalent practical experience).

Highly skilled in eliciting, documenting, and managing requirements for technical  solutions.

Exceptional analytical and problem-solving abilities

Ability to articulate complex technical concepts clearly to non-technical audiences and vice-versa, with strong presentation and negotiation skills.

A selfstarter with a strong sense of ownership and accountability, capable of  managing multiple priorities effectively

Why join us

We know people do their best work when they feel supported, trusted, and connected to purpose.

At Police Bank, you’ll find:

Meaningful work – what you do directly supports our members and community

Real impact – your ideas and contributions won’t get lost in layers

Trusted environment – we value outcomes over hierarchy

Flexibility – we support balance in how you work

Growth – opportunities to develop, stretch, and progress your career

What can we offer you?

💼 Work-Life Balance & Wellbeing
Enjoy flexible and hybrid working options, generous parental leave, and unique leave types that support your wellbeing and life circumstances. Access wellbeing programs, EAP support, and regular health initiatives.

💰 Rewards & Benefits
Benefit from competitive superannuation, salary packaging options, novated leasing, and exclusive access to financial services and employee discounts. Plus, earn through our referral program.

📚 Learning & Development
Grow your career with study support, paid study leave, professional membership reimbursement, and access to online learning resources.

🎉 Recognition & Community
Be celebrated through awards, milestone recognition, birthday perks, and volunteer leave—while contributing to a supportive and purpose-driven workplace.

What happens next?

If this role sounds like the right fit, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now!

No recruitment agencies please. We've got this. Thank you!

Police Bank is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital or disability status. This role is open to Australian and New Zealand Citizens and permanent residents only.

About the Role
 
uandu Recruitment Partners are delighted to partner exclusively with Murdoch Children’s Research Institute (MCRI) to recruit a number of essential positions within their Technology Office.
 
MCRI is investing heavily in its technology ecosystem to support increasingly data-rich research and clinical innovation. As part of this, they are seeking a Data Governance and Architecture specialist to take ownership of enterprise data frameworks, architecture, and governance practices across the organisation.
 
Reporting to the Head of Cybersecurity and Architecture, you will play a critical role in shaping how data is classified, secured, governed and leveraged across research and operational environments. This is a highly strategic and hands-on role, influencing everything from policy and standards through to solution design, implementation and continuous improvement.
 
About Murdoch Children’s Research Institute
 
Murdoch Children’s Research Institute envisions a transformative future for child health. Their institute is a hub of impactful scientific breakthroughs, particularly in early interventions, enhanced diagnosis, prevention and potential cures. They thrive on collaborative, multidisciplinary problem-solving to address challenging queries in child health.
 
Committed to inclusivity and respect, MCRI fosters a positive working environment that values diverse backgrounds and experiences. As a proud member of the Australian Network on Disability and a dedicated supporter of Science Australia Gender Equity (SAGE), the Institute upholds principles of equal opportunity and diverse representation.
 
MCRI is committed to the safety of children and young people and takes a zero-tolerance approach to all forms of child abuse, exploitation and harm. Child safety is prioritised in decision-making at all levels of the Institute.
 
Key Responsibilities

Define and evolve enterprise data architecture and governance frameworks across cloud, infrastructure and applications;
Lead data classification, labelling and data leakage prevention strategies, including tooling (e.g. Purview);
Ensure alignment with regulatory and security frameworks (ISO, NIST, AI governance);
Partner with research and business stakeholders to design fit-for-purpose, secure data solutions;
Drive governance, risk and compliance practices across data and information domains; and
Provide technical leadership across projects and contribute to the broader technology roadmap.
About You
 
You are an experienced data professional with a strong blend of architecture, governance and security expertise. You bring a pragmatic, forward-thinking approach to data in complex environments, ideally with exposure to research, healthcare or similarly regulated domains.
 
You will have:

Proven experience across data architecture, governance and data management (5+ years);
Strong knowledge of data classification, labelling and leakage detection approaches;
Experience with frameworks such as ISO 27001, NIST and emerging AI governance standards;
Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS/Azure) and modern data ecosystems; and
Strong stakeholder engagement and the ability to influence across technical and non-technical audiences.
Benefits
 
Working at MCRI offers a range of benefits including:

Access to salary packaging options including entertainment and novated leases;
Flexible working arrangements;
The opportunity to contribute to Australia’s largest child health research institute;
Generous personal leave and the option to purchase additional leave;
Discounted staff parking and excellent public transport access;
Health and wellbeing programs including Employee Assistance Program access; and
Comprehensive paid parental leave for both birth and non-birth parents.
Important Information
 
As this is an exclusive recruitment partnership, all enquiries and communication regarding this position should be directed to u&u Recruitment Partners.
 
Successful applicants will be required to undergo:

A National Police Check; and
A Working With Children Check (to be obtained at the candidate’s own expense).
How to Apply
 
For more information or a confidential discussion please contact Killian O’ Sullivan at u&u at killian.osullivan@uandu.com quoting reference number 44436.
 
At u&u Recruitment Partners, we value diversity, equity and inclusion. We welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds and people with disability.
 
Should you require reasonable adjustments or have a preferred method of communication throughout the recruitment process, we encourage you to make a request via adjustments@uandu.com or phone the above-mentioned u&u consultant to discuss. In response to these requests, we will collaborate closely with you to implement the appropriate adjustments.
 
Additionally, for a barrier-free and inclusive online experience, you can access u&u’s opportunities using accessibility software Recite Me at https://www.uandu.com/jobs.
 
Please submit your resume in Word format only.

Data Product Owner – Investment Data

Permanent, full‑time position
Flexible, hybrid working environment
Own critical investment data products in an AI‑first, highly autonomous environment

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That’s why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members.

Your New Role

Are you a Product Owner who thinks in outcomes, adoption and time‑to‑value, and isn’t afraid to experiment with AI to get there faster?

As Data Product Owner – Investment Data, you’ll sit within the Investment Data division of Data Services, partnering closely with Investment teams to shape, deliver and evolve high‑value investment data products across acquisition, mastering and production.

This role is critical to ensuring investment data is trusted, fit‑for‑purpose and consumable, supporting faster, higher‑confidence decision‑making in a complex, multi‑asset environment. You’ll work at the intersection of business, data platforms, vendors and governance, translating investment needs into clear, prioritised and actionable data products.

This is a role with genuine ownership and autonomy, ideal for someone comfortable making trade‑offs, setting priorities and holding their ground when supply is constrained and demand is high.

What You’ll Do

Own the purpose, vision and success measures for investment data products, establishing clear contracts (datasets, transformations, SLAs, controls) and minimum standards for quality, metadata, ownership, lifecycle and governance.
Shape and manage product roadmaps and prioritisation, balancing value, risk and delivery capacity, while leading fitness‑for‑purpose assessments and remediation of data sources.
Partner with internal teams and vendors to deliver reliable, well‑governed data products and support their adoption.
This role is for you if you enjoy product ownership, tough prioritisation calls, AI‑enabled ways of working, and being accountable for adoption and trust.  It’s probably not for you if you prefer policy writing without product ownership, or governance without delivery accountability.

What You’ll Need

Proven experience as a Product Owner for investment or financial data products, with strong understanding of investment operations, asset classes and front‑to‑back data flows, as well as the ability to engage credibly with Investment teams and translate complex needs into product outcomes.
Demonstrated capability in defining, governing and prioritising data products across acquisition, mastering and consumption, making effective decisions in high‑demand, constrained environments.
Technical fluency with hands‑on experience in Python, SQL and cloud‑based data patterns, enabling effective partnership with engineers.
AI‑savvy mindset with experience using AI to improve product throughput, stakeholder comms, decisioning and/or data experiences (AustralianSuper operates with an AI‑first expectation) 
Experience with IBOR concepts and downstream impacts (highly regarded), and exposure to platforms such as Informatica, LUSID or similar (desirable).
Curiosity, confidence, and the ability to ask the right questions — even when answers are uncomfortable.

Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we’re happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What’s Next

If you’re ready to bring your energy, integrity, generosity of spirit and passion for excellent outcomes to a team that loves a challenge, apply now!

Australian or New Zealand citizenship or Australian permanent residency status is required.

Progress powered by purpose.


https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

AustralianSuper may use AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead.

Our client in the resources sector is seeking a Business Analyst to act as a key partner to the System Owner. This is an initial 7-month contract position with the possibility of extension.

Your focus will be on embedding ServiceNow across the digital function, driving user adoption, and shaping the future roadmap for new modules.

What You'll Be Doing

Lead the transition from "out-of-the-box" implementation to full team adoption.

Facilitate workshops to gather system requirements and define project scope.

Overlay system functionality with human-centred workflows and process improvements.

Create training materials and facilitate sessions to ensure technical feasibility and user competence.

Develop long-term product roadmaps and assess the feasibility of additional ServiceNow modules.

Manage senior stakeholder expectations and resolve conflicts regarding process change.

What You'll Bring

A minimum of 3+ years of hands-on ServiceNow SPM experience is essential for this role.

A minimum of 5 to 6 years of proven Business Analysis experience is required.

Previous experience in Agile environments with a focus on "fit-for-purpose" workflows over rigid theory.

Proven ability to handle resistance to change and lead stakeholders through system transitions.

Facilitation skills and the ability to translate complex insights into clear service concepts.

Must possess full work rights and must be based in Perth, WA.

Just imagine a future with us...

At Aurecon, Infrastructure Advisory goes beyond traditional consulting. We combine strategy, commercial insight and technical depth to help clients make confident, future-focused investment decisions.

Join a growing national team working at the intersection of strategy, investment and delivery - shaping infrastructure that drives real economic and community impact.

What will you do?

As a Senior Consultant, you'll play a hands-on role across the full infrastructure lifecycle, from early investment thinking through to delivery. You will:

Deliver high-quality advisory across business cases, feasibility studies, due diligence and investment strategies
Develop client-ready outputs including option assessments, technical reports and financial models (e.g. CBA, lifecycle and whole-of-life costing)
Work across diverse sectors including transport, water, energy, resources, social infrastructure and defence
Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams across advisory, engineering and design
Build trusted relationships with clients and stakeholders
Contribute to business growth through opportunity shaping, client engagement and proposal development
Support team capability, mentoring junior consultants and contributing to a high-performing, inclusive culture

What are we looking for from you?

We're seeking commercially minded, curious professionals who thrive in complexity and bring a balance of strategic thinking and delivery focus. You will bring:

Relevant tertiary qualifications (e.g. economics, finance, engineering, commerce, planning)
Experience in infrastructure advisory, consulting or related environments
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate complex inputs into clear, practical advice
Well-developed communication skills and confidence engaging with senior stakeholders
Ability to manage competing priorities across multiple projects
A collaborative, adaptable mindset and commitment to quality outcomes

At Aurecon, we value diverse experiences and perspectives. You'll be part of a team that's passionate about shaping better infrastructure outcomes and creating a more inclusive, innovative future.

Ready to bring ideas to life? Apply now.

Business Analyst
6‑month contract
ASAP start
Brisbane | Hybrid (2–3 days onsite)

About the Role
We’re partnering with a large Queensland organisation embarking on a significant Pay Integrity uplift. This program is focused on strengthening payroll accuracy, compliance, governance and assurance across key people and pay processes.
You’ll play a central role in redesigning business processes, uplifting system controls, and supporting the delivery of multiple Power Platform applications and integrations. This is a highly collaborative role working across HR, Payroll, Finance, Governance, Digital teams and an external delivery partner to ensure solutions are accurate, auditable and aligned to organisational standards.
This is a hands‑on BA role suited to someone who thrives in complex environments, enjoys structured analysis, and can bridge business, technical and governance perspectives.

Key Responsibilities
Lead structured engagement with HR, Payroll, Finance, Governance and technical teams to elicit and analyse requirements related to pay integrity, compliance, reporting and assurance.
Translate business needs into clear, testable functional and non‑functional requirements for Power Platform applications, workflows, integrations and reporting.
Develop high‑quality BA artefacts including user stories, acceptance criteria, process models, data definitions, security requirements and configuration specifications.
Support the design and implementation of role‑based access controls, data security, privacy and audit requirements.
Work closely with architects, developers and testers to validate solutions and ensure alignment with enterprise standards.
Support system, integration and user acceptance testing, including defect triage and resolution.
Contribute to go‑live readiness activities including requirements traceability, documentation, stakeholder preparation and transition planning.
Provide ongoing analysis and support through delivery, go‑live and early hypercare.

Skills & Experience Needed
Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst delivering complex business or digital solutions within HR, payroll, finance or compliance‑driven environments.
Strong capability in requirements elicitation, analysis and documentation (user stories, acceptance criteria, traceability).
Experience working with or alongside low‑code platforms such as Power Apps, Power Automate and Power BI.
Solid process modelling skills with the ability to produce clear, fit‑for‑purpose process and data artefacts.
Experience defining or supporting security, access control, privacy and audit requirements.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to bridge business, technical and vendor teams.
High‑level analytical and problem‑solving capability.
Experience using JIRA and Confluence for requirements and delivery management.

Bonus Points For
Prior experience in payroll compliance, pay integrity uplift, or HR/payroll process redesign.
Exposure to governance‑heavy environments or audit remediation projects.
Experience supporting multi‑application Power Platform delivery.
Background working in large, complex or public‑sector organisations.

Product Analyst – Workforce Planning & Industrial Instruments
6 month contract + strong chance of extension
Major Australian Organisation 
Based in Hawthorn - 3 days in office

We are seeking an experienced Product Analyst to support workforce planning products and initiatives, with a strong focus on Enterprise Agreements (EBAs), awards, wage rate changes, allowances, and industrial compliance. This role plays a critical part in ensuring workforce, payroll, and rostering solutions accurately reflect complex industrial arrangements and support effective planning and decision‑making.

You will work closely with product managers, workforce planning teams, payroll, HR, industrial relations, and technology teams to translate industrial requirements into clear product insights, data models, and functional requirements.

In this role you will:
Analyse and interpret Enterprise Agreements, awards, wage structures, allowances and penalty conditions to support workforce planning and related products.
Translate industrial and workforce requirements into clear functional specifications, user stories and acceptance criteria.
Support product development related to labour cost modelling, workforce forecasting, and compliance controls.
Work with payroll and workforce systems to ensure accurate implementation of rate changes, allowance rules, and award conditions.
Analyse the impact of EBA changes and industrial updates on workforce planning, costs, and operational outcomes.
Collaborate with data, technology and business teams to validate calculations, rules logic, and reporting outputs.
Support testing and validation activities to ensure workforce and pay‑related changes are implemented correctly.
Provide ongoing analysis and insights to support continuous improvement of workforce planning products.
To be successful in this role you will have:
Proven experience as a Product Analyst, Business Analyst or Workforce Analyst in workforce planning, payroll, or industrial relations environments.
Strong practical understanding of Enterprise Agreements, modern awards, wage rates, allowances, penalties and compliance requirements.
Experience working with workforce planning, rostering, payroll or HR systems.
Ability to interpret complex industrial documentation and translate it into clear, implementable requirements.
Strong analytical skills with attention to detail, particularly around calculations and rules‑based logic.
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to work across HR, IR, payroll, finance and technology teams.
Experience working in Agile or product‑led delivery environments is highly desirable.

The SunRice Group is an Australian ASX-listed branded food group, comprising multiple businesses, assets and operations across Australia, New Zealand, the Middle East, the United States, The Pacific Islands and Asia.  SunRice was built by the Australian rice industry over many decades and today is one of Australia’s leading branded food exporters, with approximately 2,000 employees and operations in 10 countries.

SunRice is a great place to work, providing a real opportunity for passionate people to make a difference.

 

About the role

At SunRice, everything starts with our consumers - and this role sits right at the heart of that belief.

We’re looking for a Senior Insights Manager to lead insights across our Rice portfolio in ANZ, helping us better understand how people shop, cook and enjoy our food every day. As our insights subject matter expert and thought leader, you’ll uncover what really matters to consumers and shoppers, and translate that into clear direction for our brands, innovation and commercial plans.

You’ll partner closely with Marketing, Sales, R&D, using insight to bring people on the journey - from the data, to the “so what”, to confident decisions that fuel growth. This is a 12‑month fixed‑term maternity leave cover and a great opportunity to step into a visible role where curiosity, collaboration and storytelling are just as important as analytical rigour.

 

Key responsibilities will include:

Owning consumer, shopper and market insights for the Rice portfolio across ANZ

Leading end‑to‑end qualitative and quantitative research, turning insight into clear direction for the business

Ad Text:
Senior Quantitative Analyst: Model Risk & Markets


Leading Bank
Permanent Role
Model Risk Team



The Company




A major Australian financial institution is seeking a Senior Quantitative Analyst to join its Markets Model Risk team in Sydney. This is a permanent, technical role focused on ensuring that valuation, risk, and capital models are mathematically sound and fit for their intended purpose.

You will work within a high-performing environment that prioritizes technical quality and independent challenge across trading and banking books.




The Role



Independent Model Assessment: Evaluate valuation, risk, and capital models across various asset classes, specifically covering interest rates, foreign exchange, and commodities.
Technical Validation: Review and test models used across Financial Markets, Risk, and Treasury to ensure they are robust and well-understood.
Stakeholder Engagement: Partner with a range of business units to explain complex modeling outcomes and technical findings to senior leadership.
Risk Oversight: Support Group-wide decision-making by managing risks associated with model assumptions and market activity.
Project Contribution: Participate in critical modeling projects that impact the broader technical framework of the organization.

Your Experience

Professional Experience: At least 4-5 years of experience working with or assessing financial markets valuation, risk, or capital models.
Technical Knowledge: A solid understanding of derivatives and models related to FX and interest rates.
Programming Proficiency: Practical experience in programming languages such as C++, R, or similar technical tools.
Education: A tertiary qualification in a quantitative discipline (e.g., Mathematics, Physics, or Statistics).
Communication: The ability to simplify complex technical topics and the confidence to constructively challenge existing model assumptions.

We are looking to hire a Data Modeller who will focus on translating complex business requirements into clear, scalable, conceptual, logical, and physical data models that support analytics, reporting, and operational needs. Your future role and responsibilities:

Contribute to the design, development, and continuous refinement of conceptual, logical, and physical data models within the Data Transformation team.

Create and maintain SQL DDL scripts, along with detailed mapping and ETL documentation, to support Data Engineers in constructing and loading data models.

When required, carry out reverse engineering of existing data models from databases or SQL code to ensure alignment with current architecture and standards.

Develop and update business documentation, including process maps, taxonomies, and ontology diagrams, to provide clarity and traceability of data flows.

Maintain a strong emphasis on conceptual and business data modelling, ensuring that structures align with organisational objectives.

Assist in managing and maintaining controlled vocabularies and the corporate data catalogue to promote consistency and reuse of data assets.

Experience required to be successful in this role:

Sound understanding of data modelling methodologies, including Kimball, Inmon, Top-down/Bottom-up, Relational and Dimensional Modelling, Data Warehousing, and 3NF approaches.

Ability to think conceptually and apply modelling techniques such as generalisation, subtyping, and super-typing to create efficient and flexible models.

Skilled in producing Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) using a range of notations, such as Crow’s Foot and UML.

Strong technical understanding of databases, ETL/ELT pipelines, and programming languages (typically SQL), with the ability to connect these technologies to data modelling practices. (This is a hands-on role involving active work with data.)

Solid comprehension of business processes, with the ability to capture requirements accurately and translate them into effective technical designs.

Confident communicator, capable of engaging in technical discussions with both technical and non-technical audiences across all organisational levels.

Experience with cloud-based data technologies, particularly within the Microsoft Azure ecosystem, including:

Ad Text:
Our client a large, complex statutory body is seeking a proactive and highly organised Change Analyst to support the delivery of a large scale transformation.

This is a significant, multi-year transformation program that will touch a large part of the organisation. While the broader position description references change delivery, this particular role is more heavily focused on change analysis, coordination, reporting and program-level visibility.

You will support the Change Manager and broader program team by maintaining key change artefacts, analysing stakeholder and SME information, supporting communication activity, and helping provide structure across a complex transformation environment.

About the role

This role would suit someone who enjoys detail, structure and analysis. You will be responsible for supporting the smooth running of change activity across the program, including maintaining registers, tracking engagement, coordinating inputs and helping ensure key information is visible and actionable.
Key activities will include:

Maintaining stakeholder and SME registers at program level
Supporting stakeholder mapping and engagement planning
Coordinating change inputs across multiple initiatives
Supporting communication calendars, intranet updates and program communications
Preparing PowerPoint packs, Excel trackers and reporting artefacts
Conducting analysis and making recommendations around engagement, communications and change activity
Supporting change impact assessments, feedback analysis, readiness activity and adoption tracking
Assisting with workshop logistics and stakeholder engagement as the program moves into later design phases

About you
You will bring a mix of change analysis, project coordination and transformation support experience. 

Experience supporting change activity across complex programs or projects
Strong Excel, PowerPoint, documentation and reporting skills
Experience maintaining stakeholder registers, comms calendars, action logs or program trackers
Strong attention to detail and the ability to synthesise information
Confidence engaging with stakeholders and SMEs
A proactive, self-sufficient and “get things done” approach
Exposure to change impact assessments, stakeholder analysis, communications or readiness activity
Experience in transformation, technology, digital, insurance, government or complex service environments would be beneficial
Prosci or equivalent change certification is desirable, but not essential. The formal position description also highlights stakeholder mapping, impact analysis, change reporting, feedback analysis and adoption tracking as key responsibilities.

Job Title: Associate Director – Group Treasury (Analytics and Innovation)
Ad Text:
About us

Resimac Group is a dynamic and leading ASX-listed non-bank lender and multi-channel distribution business in Australia and New Zealand.  We enable homeowners and businesses to achieve their dreams by providing innovative, fast and accessible lending solutions to homeowners, consumers and business across Australia and New Zealand. 

Our brands include Resimac, Resimac NZ, homeloans.com.au and Resimac Asset Finance and we’re proud to be servicing over 50,000+ current customers, with assets under management of over $15 billion, and a fast-growing workforce of over 330 people operating across Australia, New Zealand and the Philippines.




Why You’ll Love This Role

This is a rare and highly visible opportunity to shape the future of Group Treasury by leading its analytics, forecasting and innovation agenda end-to-end. You will work closely with the Group Treasurer, senior executives and enterprise-wide technology teams to deliver smarter, faster and more predictive treasury insights.

You’ll gain exposure to:

End-to-end Treasury analytics across funding, capital, securitisation and trust management
Enterprise AI, data and platform transformation initiatives
Complex RMBS and ABS transactions and capital markets analysis
Strategic funding, capital and balance sheet optimisation
Senior stakeholder engagement across Finance, Risk, Technology and the Executive team

This role is ideal for a technically strong and commercially minded leader who enjoys operating at the intersection of Treasury, data and innovation.

 

The Role

As Associate Director - Group Treasury (Analytics & Innovation), you will be responsible for all aspects of Treasury data, analytics and forecasting. You will lead the design and delivery of analytical frameworks that support funding strategy, capital management, securitisation execution and enterprise decision-making.

You will also play a key role in identifying and implementing automation, digital and AI initiatives to drive efficiency, productivity and scalability across Treasury and Trust Management functions.

Key responsibilities include:

Treasury Analytics & Forecasting

Review and enhance monthly and ad‑hoc Treasury and Finance forecasting and modelling
Lead cost of funds, capital, debt and working capital forecasting
Develop and maintain product-level pricing and profitability models
Deliver portfolio risk analysis and stress testing insights

Funding, Securitisation & Capital Analytics

Provide analytical support for warehouse funding and bank facility optimisation
Lead modelling and analysis for RMBS and ABS transactions, including cashflow, WAL, credit and capital structures
Support analysis and recommendations on currency, duration and transaction structuring
Deliver investor, dealer and ratings agency analytics and reporting

Innovation, Data & Automation

Identify, design and implement data, digital and AI initiatives across Treasury and Trust Management
Participate in enterprise-wide AI and analytics initiatives
Lead implementation of new analytics platforms and tools
Review, rebuild and relaunch the debt investor portal
Identify automation opportunities to enhance reporting, controls and external disclosures

Strategic Projects & M&A

Provide valuation, return and capital impact analysis for M&A opportunities
Support enterprise strategy development relating to data, forecasting and analytics
Deliver funding and capital recommendations for growth initiatives

 

About You

You are a senior treasury or analytics professional with a strong background in financial modelling, data analytics and capital markets. You enjoy solving complex problems, challenging existing processes and using data to influence strategic outcomes.

You are comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders, confident translating complex data into clear insights, and motivated by driving continuous improvement through technology and innovation.

Skills & Experience

Extensive experience within Treasury, capital markets, securitisation or financial services
Deep expertise in financial modelling, forecasting and analytics
Strong understanding of warehouse funding, RMBS and ABS structures
Experience supporting capital, funding and balance sheet strategy
Advanced technical skills including Power BI, Tableau or similar BI tools
Advanced Excel and SQL capability
Experience delivering data, digital, automation or AI initiatives (highly valued)
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Attributes

Highly analytical with strong commercial judgement
Strategic thinker with the ability to execute and deliver outcomes
Curious and innovation-oriented mindset
Detail-focused while able to see the bigger picture
Confident presenting insights and recommendations to senior leadership
Collaborative and relationship-driven

About us

Children’s Hospital Foundation funds cutting-edge equipment, vital research and on the ground support for kids with all illnesses, injuries and conditions, throughout Queensland and northern New South Wales. We identify and action gaps in funding and support right across the kids’ health landscape – the places left lacking but no less deserving.

Be the difference

Are you ready to be part of something special? It is an exciting time to join the Children’s Hospital Foundation as we embark on a period of transformation to achieve our ambitious five-year strategy. We aren’t just evolving; we are revolutionising how we make a difference to sick kids across Queensland.

We value who you are, not just your skillset. We are looking for people who purposeful, wholehearted, collaborative, curious and courageous. People who are prepared to lead from where they stand and who are excited to build it with us.   

We are shaking things up and transforming how we operate, how we raise funds and how we deliver the greatest impact for sick and injured children and young people across Queensland. 

If this sounds like something you would like to be a part of, then we want to meet you! 

The role

The CRM Analyst plays a critical role in supporting and enhancing Children’s Hospital Foundation’s fundraising performance through effective CRM operations, reporting and data insights. Working closely with the Head of Mass Fundraising & Data Strategy and the Fundraising Operations Manager, this role ensures the stability, governance and optimisation of Dynamics 365 and related fundraising systems, while delivering meaningful analysis and reporting to inform decision‑making across the Mass Fundraising team. Acting as a key bridge between data, systems and fundraising delivery, the CRM Analyst enables data‑led supporter engagement, campaign effectiveness and revenue growth.

This is a full‑time, permanent role based at our corporate office in South Brisbane.

Who are you?

A data‑driven professional who enjoys solving complex problems and improving systems

Purpose‑led, with a genuine interest in using data to support meaningful fundraising outcomes

Confident working with CRM platforms and translating data insights for non‑technical stakeholders

Comfortable challenging systems and processes to drive continuous improvement

Organised and detail‑focused, with a strong commitment to data quality and governance

A collaborative team player who contributes positively to a supportive and inclusive culture

Key criteria

To be successful in this role, you will possess the following:

Tertiary or vocational qualification in IT, Data Analytics, or a related discipline, or an equivalent combination of relevant experience and/or education.

Microsoft certifications preferred including relevant Power Platform, Dynamics, Azure, and Power BI (other IT industry certifications considered)

3+ years’ hands‑on experience supporting and administering CRM systems, ideally Microsoft Dynamics 365

Strong data analysis capability, including writing and optimising SQL queries for reporting and insights

Demonstrated experience designing and delivering reports and dashboards using Power BI, including data modelling

Experience working with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake, Microsoft Fabric, Azure Synapse or similar

Proven experience supporting system improvements, integrations and continuous optimisation initiatives

Experience administering or developing solutions within the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps)

Strong communication skills, with the ability to translate data insights into clear and actionable information for non‑technical stakeholders

Experience working with vendors and external technology partners

A collaborative approach, with the ability to work effectively across fundraising, operations and IT stakeholders

Experience in a not‑for‑profit or fundraising environment (desirable)

What is our difference?

When you join the team, you’re not just doing another ‘job’. You are playing your part, bringing about real change, creating the difference every kid deserves. Our team is passionate, inclusive, fun and caring. You will also be able to access a range of benefits and programs to help you be the best version of you, including:

A market-competitive salary package

3 bonus days of annual leave 

NFP salary packaging benefits

Work/life balance through flexible and hybrid working

Learning and development opportunities

Free, confidential counselling and wellbeing support for you and your family

Corporate health care plan

Modern open plan office overlooking South Bank

Copy and paste the below link into your web browser, select apply for this job, complete the application form and upload your resume and a cover letter outlining your interest and suitability.

A2B Australia is part of the ComfortDelGro Australia (CDC) Group, the country’s leading taxi network and a major provider of mobility, technology, and payment solutions.

Through our iconic brands 13cabs, Silver Service, Swan Taxis and Cabcharge we deliver end‑to‑end transport experiences, from booking and dispatch through to safe, reliable travel and seamless payment. Our services support corporate, community and disability customers nationwide.
Our network is Australia’s largest, with 7,000+ vehicles and 11,500 drivers, supported by contact centres, technology hubs and in‑field support teams. We power drivers, operators and passengers with dispatch, navigation, in‑car tech, apps and payment solutions across Australia and globally.
  
The A2B Way
We care about our customers, our business and each other. We work as one connected team, value accountability and collaboration, and encourage innovation to shape the future of mobility. We invest in wellbeing, safety, learning and development, recognise high performance, and celebrate diversity and inclusion.

The Role

We’re looking for a talented Insight Engineer to join our growing data team within a fast‑paced taxi and payments business. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and emerging AI, playing a critical part in turning complex data into trusted, actionable insights that support smarter business decisions.

Working closely with business stakeholders, analysts, and data scientists, you’ll design and deliver scalable data pipelines, translate business needs into robust data models, and enable advanced analytics and AI use cases. This is a hands‑on, high‑impact role where your work will directly influence how the business operates, grows, and innovates. 

What you’ll be doing

Design, build, and maintain scalable Azure data pipelines and platforms across Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Lake
Develop and optimise data models supporting data warehouse, big data, and Data Vault architectures
Deliver reliable batch and streaming data solutions, ensuring high data quality and performance
Translate business requirements into curated datasets and semantic layers that power reporting, analytics, and decision‑making
Partner closely with stakeholders to generate actionable insights from complex datasets such as payments, trips, and customer behaviour
Implement CI/CD and infrastructure‑as‑code practices to improve reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency
Support emerging AI and advanced analytics use cases, working with data scientists on feature engineering and model deployment (where applicable).
About you
 
We’re looking for an experienced Insight Engineer who has operated within enterprise‑scale organisations, managing complex data environments and working across multiple stakeholders. Our ideal candidate brings experience in data or analytics engineering, with strong hands‑on expertise across the Azure data stack and a solid grounding in data warehousing, data modelling, and ETL/ELT best practices. You’re comfortable working with high‑volume, business‑critical data, translating insight requirements into robust technical solutions, and partnering closely with the business to deliver reliable, scalable outcomes.

Skills and experience

Required 

5–7+ years experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or insight engineering roles  
Strong hands-on experience with Azure data stack:  
Azure Data Factory  
Azure Synapse Analytics  
Azure Data Lake
Solid understanding of:  
Data warehousing concepts (star schema, dimensional modelling)  
Big data processing frameworks  
Data Vault modelling 
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Stored procedure, PowerShell,  
Experience with real-time/streaming data (e.g., Kafka, Event Hubs)  
At least one programming language (Python preferred)  
Experience building and maintaining robust ETL/ELT pipelines  
Strong understanding of business data and ability to translate insight requirements into technical solutions  
Ready to apply?

If you’re an experienced Insight Engineer looking to work in a complex, enterprise environment and make a meaningful impact through data, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

Team leader position 
Hybrid working model (2 days working from home & 3 days working in the office)

 

Compass Group is listed on the London Stock Exchange (Top 20) and is the global market leader in providing food and a range of support services to customers in the workplace, schools and colleges, hospitals, at leisure and in remote environments. We operate in over 50 countries, employ over 600,000 people, and are a recognized top 10 employer worldwide. In Australia, Compass Group Australia (CGA) generates $2.4Bn revenue per annum, servicing over 350 clients across over 700 sites, with 15,000 employees.

Join Compass Group and play a key role in shaping high-quality, sustainable food solutions across our diverse client portfolios. In this role, you will lead structured SKU and menu optimisation initiatives, combining culinary expertise with data-driven insight to deliver measurable value for our clients. Working closely with culinary, procurement, and sector stakeholders, you will translate complex analysis into practical recommendations that support smarter decision-making and long-term outcomes.
 
About the Role

As Product Analyst - Optimisation Team Lead, you will be a key team member of Central Culinary' team, plus a critical part in delivering incremental financial and non-financial benefits to member clients through data-driven analysis, culinary insight, and strong stakeholder engagement.

You will act as the key conduit between Procurement, Culinary teams, and Compass Group Australia sectors, supporting menu development, tender responses, and optimisation initiatives while leading a small national culinary services team.

Key Responsibilities
Perform net pricing, scenario, leakage, and inflation analysis. 
Lead end-to-end SKU optimisation programs, from opportunity identification through to consultation, approval, and delivery
Deliver structured reporting on optimisation performance, benefits realised, and initiatives in progress
Provide culinary and commercial expertise to support product rationalisation, optimisation, and alternate recommendations
Partner with procurement teams on optimised product specifications for tenders and sourcing activities
Work closely with sector culinary teams to develop and validate menus
Manage national stakeholder relationships with Sector GMs and Executive Directors
Lead and develop a team of culinary services professionals
Support business development activity with commercial data, modelling, and supplier collateral

 

What You’ll Bring

Essential:

Strong commercial and analytical capability with experience working with complex data
Sound understanding of pricing, costing, and commercial principles
Advanced Excel skills (financial modelling, pivots, lookups)
Clear, professional communication skills and strong stakeholder engagement capability
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver to timeframes

Desirable:

Experience in procurement, sourcing, contracting, and culinary environments
Exposure to FMCG, food, catering, or hospitality industries
Experience with tools such as Tableau, SQL, or Access
Relevant tertiary qualification (STEM, Finance, Economics, Supply Chain or similar)

 

Ready to make a meaningful impact while advancing your career? Apply now and be part of something bigger. You’ll collaborate with culinary and cross-functional stakeholders to interpret insights, share outcomes clearly, and support smarter, faster decision-making across product and menu optimisation. Join Compass Group’s Culinary team and help deliver sustainable, client-focused food solutions.

 

Working with a large global company means you can be assured that we have an attractive benefits program! This includes comprehensive learning & development offerings, a variety of recognition programs and company discounts, an excellent salary benchmarking matrix and at this management level also includes an incentive bonus scheme! You can read all about these here: Why work at Compass

We are committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you require adjustments or alternative methods of communication in the recruitment process, please reach out to us on either talisson@compass-group.com.au or georgefouskarinis@compass-group.com.au

Initial 6-month contract + extensions
Lean Six Sigma Black Belt certification required
Location: CBD- Hybrid work model
Our client is looking for a Senior Process Analyst to join a large AI transformation initiative.

Working closely with business stakeholders, you will carry out 'As Is' analysis and identify how to optimise the process and implement a solution to uplift efficiency.  You will be involved in the design of the future target state, as well as the optimisation and uplift of productivity. You'll be supporting business outcomes, around how to reduce leakage and improve outcomes in the process.

Key Responsibilities:

Conduct ‘As Is analysis
Implement a business process management framework to systematically document processes including process mapping, defining key performance indicators and controls and identifying risks.
Essential skills and experience

Previous experience as a Process Analyst
Strong business process transformation experience
Must have Lean Six Sigma Black Belt certification (mandatory).
Experience delivering large-scale, enterprise-wide transformation
Ability to lead workshops and facilitate senior stakeholders
Work strategically (future-state design) AND hands-on (process mapping)
Experience within financial services is highly desirable
For more info contact Aparna on Aparna.sharma@peoplebank.com.au

This is a rare opportunity for a proven executive leader to step into a high-impact role driving large-scale programs across government, industry and research.
 
About This Role
This is a rare opportunity to step into a critical executive role with a national mandate, leading a portfolio that sits at the centre of government engagement, industry engagement across the energy and VET sectors, workforce strategy, and research.
The organisation is entering its next phase of maturity, and this role is pivotal in shaping how strategy is translated into coordinated, system-wide delivery across the entire portfolio.
You will take over a well-established function and be expected to build on strong foundations, bringing credibility, authority and a clear strategic voice to both internal and external stakeholders.
This is not just a relationship or advisory role - success will come from someone who can think at a systems level, communicate a clear vision, and demonstrate how they have personally led and delivered complex, national programs with measurable impact.
 
Why This Role Matters
This portfolio plays a central role in:

Influencing government policy and strengthening national relationships
Driving workforce planning, strategy and sector-wide insights
Integrating research, data and industry engagement into actionable outcomes
Lifting organisational capability and aligning delivery across a national footprint
Leading tripartite engagement across government, industry and unions to drive aligned outcomes
 As part of the executive team, you will help shape the organisation’s strategic direction, credibility and long-term impact.
 

Key Benefits

National executive role with direct influence on policy, workforce strategy and sector outcomes
Opportunity to lead a critical, high-visibility portfolio at the centre of government, industry and research
Work closely with the CEO, Executive and Board, contributing to enterprise-wide strategy and direction
Lead and develop a high-calibre, multi-disciplinary team, shaping capability at scale
Drive large-scale, nationally significant programs with measurable economic and workforce impact
Play a key role in the organisation’s next phase of maturity, structure and influence
 
Key Responsibilities

Lead a national portfolio, aligning government, energy and VET industry engagement, research and workforce initiatives
Drive strategic government engagement, strengthening policy influence and key relationships
Lead industry and union engagement, operating effectively within a tripartite model
Provide clear thought leadership, setting direction and articulating how outcomes will be delivered
Lead and uplift a senior leadership team, building capability and accountability across the function
Translate systems thinking into execution, connecting strategy to measurable results
Oversee workforce insights and research, ensuring outputs are rigorous, relevant and designed to influence policy and industry outcomes
Strengthen internal capability, ensuring consistent delivery across the full portfolio
Contribute to financial strategy and decision-making, linking investment to impact
Deliver high-quality submissions, briefings and strategic outputs for executive and Board audiences
 
What You’ll Bring

Proven experience leading enterprise-level, national programs or portfolios of significant scale and complexity
Background in federal/state government, national NFPs, universities or industry bodies delivering sector-wide impact
Demonstrated ability to operate as a systems leader, translating strategy into coordinated, multi-stream delivery
Highly developed executive communication skills, able to articulate vision, direction and outcomes with clarity and authority
Clear track record of owning and solving complex problems end-to-end, with tangible outcomes
Credibility and presence to influence senior stakeholders, including government, industry and union representatives
Strong financial and commercial acumen, with confidence contributing to investment and funding decisions
Experience leading senior, multi-disciplinary teams, with the ability to challenge, influence and uplift experienced leaders
A forward-thinking, strategic mindset, comfortable operating in ambiguity and driving change
 
This is a genuinely unique role suited to a proven executive leader already operating at, or ready to step into, General Manager level, with the ability to bring authority, structure and strategic clarity to a critical function.
 
If you think this could be the role for you then please apply with your CV and Cover Letter.

Salary: Base + super + corporate bonus plan + perks
Ad Text:
Why Smart?

Look forward to working with Australia's leading and most trusted provider of Employee Management Services, making the benefits of salary packaging, novated leasing, and fleet management readily available to employers and their employees.

Working at Smart offers more than a job. It is a dynamic workplace that matches the enthusiasm for the work you do and ambition for an exciting fast-paced career. We have all been hired for a purpose and a reason.

Every day you will be part of a team that helps support fellow Australians - many working in essential services, corporates, government, and not-for-profits - to take home more money in their pay packets.

Do you have what it takes to become a Data Governance Lead?

This is a career-defining role for a hands-on Data Governance leader who thrives in ambiguity and knows how to drive real outcomes — not just frameworks.

You will step into a team undergoing change, uplift capability, continue building credibility with stakeholders, and deliver pragmatic, tangible outcomes that demonstrate why data governance matters.

This role suits someone who combines strong technical depth with people leadership, can confidently challenge senior stakeholders, and is comfortable rolling up their sleeves to get things done.


What you'll do


• Own and deliver a Data Governance & Management roadmap with tangible, measurable outcomes
• Drive adoption of data governance
• Partner with senior stakeholders to embed data ownership and stewardship
• Deliver pragmatic governance standards, processes and data artefacts (glossary, dictionary, lineage, CDEs)
• Improve data quality through hands-on profiling, remediation and controls
• Report on governance and data quality metrics to senior leadership and board forums

What we're looking for


• 5+ years' experience across multiple data management disciplines (Data Governance, Data Quality, Data Risk, Data Modelling)
• Hands-on experience implementing data governance tools and catalogues
• Technical experience with: Azure Purview, Databricks (Unity Catalog), SQL, Data catalogues, data quality and metadata management tools
• Strong people leadership experience
• Excellent stakeholder management skills with the confidence to challenge and influence
• A pragmatic, outcomes-driven mindset — comfortable being hands-on
• Experience working in Agile delivery environments

What's in it for you?

Smart team members enjoy a range of benefits designed to support flexibility, wellbeing, reward, and growth:

Smart Leave, an additional week of leave each year
Free lunch every week
Up to 20 weeks Paid Parental Leave with superannuation contributions and a Return-to-Work Bonus
Make a Difference Days - paid days off to volunteer for the community and environment
Discounted retail vouchers
Salary packaging and novated leasing benefits (of course!)
Free flu vaccinations
Employee Share Plan

Grow with Purpose

At Smart, we're driven by purpose and performance. We support individual and team growth via varied learning opportunities, open communication, and leaders who work to get the basics right - quality and regular one to ones, clear goals and success metrics, and growth conversations.




Show us you have what it takes

If this sounds like the opportunity for you and you have something to offer, we'd love to hear from you. APPLY ONLINE today.
Our Talent Acquisition team will be in touch to discuss next steps. If you require any adjustments to the recruitment process or are seeking part-time work, let us know—we're here to understand.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

We're committed to Equality, Diversity and Inclusivity. We welcome all people to join our team — including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, veterans, people with disability, people who identify as LGBTQ+, all gender identities, people of any socioeconomic status, age, race, national origin or ethnicity.

We're proud to hold diversity and inclusion accreditations, including:
- WGEA Employer of Choice for Gender Equality since 2019
- DCA Inclusive Employer 2023-2024
- Member of Reconciliation Australia's RAP network

Please note: all successful candidates will be required to complete background checks including a police clearance prior to confirmation of employment. Candidates must have unlimited Australian work rights to be considered for this role.

Respectfully, no agencies please.

Job Title: Program Change Analyst – Claims Transformation Program
Salary: No contractor fees / two pay runs per week
Ad Text:
Program Change Analyst – Claims Transformation Program

6-Month Contract | Brisbane (Hybrid) | Large-Scale Transformation Program

Role Overview

We are seeking a capable Change Analyst to join a major enterprise-wide transformation program within a well-established Queensland organisation.

This role will operate at the program level, supporting multiple workstreams and providing critical visibility across change activities. It is a highly analytical and coordination-focused role, suited to someone who enjoys working with data, stakeholders, and structured artefacts to drive informed decision-making.

While aligned to change management, this role is less focused on end-to-end change delivery and more centred on analysis, reporting, and program coordination.

Key Responsibilities

Develop, maintain, and analyse program-level stakeholder registers (including SMEs and key stakeholders)

Consolidate inputs from multiple initiatives to provide centralised visibility of change impacts and activities

Support the development and management of communication plans, calendars, and intranet content

Conduct stakeholder and engagement analysis, providing insights and recommendations to support effective communication strategies

Assist with program reporting and governance artefacts

Support workshop coordination and logistics, particularly as the program moves into design phases

Work closely with the Change Lead to ensure alignment and consistency across program-level change activities

What We’re Looking For

Proven experience as a Change Analyst, Change Coordinator, or Project Coordinator within complex environments

Strong analytical capability, with the ability to interpret data and provide meaningful insights

Advanced skills in Excel and PowerPoint (essential)

Experience supporting large-scale transformation or program environments

Ability to work proactively and autonomously, with a strong attention to detail

Excellent organisational and coordination skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities

Strong communication skills, with the ability to support stakeholder engagement activities

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Awarded Australia’s top-performing small agency in 2021 and 2022, with a finalist spot in 2023. Partner with Lime for genuine, experienced, friendly recruiters dedicated to securing the best opportunities for you. Apply now or contact  Ianthe Price on 0481 360 333 or ianthe@limejobs.com.au to find out more!

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions.

What you’ll be doing:

The Finance Manager, Group Financial Planning & Analysis role is to be responsible for supporting the FP&A leadership team to deliver on the strategic and financial goals of the group. This will be delivered by aligning the finance function with business activity, underpinning budgets and forecasts, through to the impact of cash flow, and the variable and fixed components of the cost base over the cycle. The role involves reporting and providing insights on a regular basis to identify forecast risks and performance.

This role is a 14 month, maximum term contract (you will still have access to annual leave, sick leave and our wonderful travel benefits). The position reports into the Head of Financial Planning & Analysis and this role has 2 direct reports.

Further responsibilities include:

Oversee the preparation and presentation of the group management accounts for Board, executive member and their leadership team discussions including supporting the executive in reviewing the financial performance of their leadership team’s departments
Oversee the preparation and provision of group quarterly reforecasts and any recurring structured budget development, ensuring accurate and appropriate use of key business performance metrics and relevant function owner support
Ownership of the Group’s Capital Planning Process, including management of the governance process and benefits tracking
Ownership and oversight as the senior business partner for the Corporate executives including Finance, Legal, Risk, People, Strategy and Corporate Affairs & Sustainability.
Support the Group Reporting Team for statutory reporting including, Impairment paper, Going Concern and CFO supporting notes
Provision of ACCC, Financier and Shareholder Quarterly reporting
Develop effective and influencing working relationships across all levels of management and with the wider FP&A team
Build and validate complex business cases in connection with business transformation, ensuring to justify potential investment and quantifying benefit, in support of the company’s growth strategy
Leadership of the Financial Planning and Analysis teams both your direct reports and the wider team
Assess and implement the most appropriate team structure, ensuring team leaders have the right leadership skills and subject matter capability for their respective functions
Build and maintain effective relationships within and across the business units & departments
Proactively work with internal stakeholders across the VA Group and external stakeholders, as required

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

CA/ CPA or equivalent qualifications
Tertiary qualifications in Business/ Economics/Commercial
Extensive experience in business partnering and finance support of business units or the wider Finance team
Experience within a large, publicly listed, corporate business
Extensive leadership experience
Extensive experience in developing forecast models and complex business cases
Development of KPIs and performance huddles for new business proposals
Proven track record in improving processes and delivering against deadlines
Experience in dealing with external stakeholders
Ability to think strategically and implement initiatives in a way that delivers business outcomes
Excellent communication skills and ability to provide concise accurate and professional reports and commentary
You are Brisbane based or willing to relocate to Brisbane.

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to):

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (including $1000 worth of travel credits per year)
A flexible, hybrid work style. Work from home or the office, adapt your location to suit your style and needs
One of Brisbane’s most admired head office locations, with South Bank train station, a supermarket, pharmacy, and lunch options downstairs plus a 5-minute walk to the Busway, a rooftop restaurant and onsite gym (with a low membership fee) and South Bank Parklands just across the road
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services
Free counselling and access to a wellbeing app to support your physical, mental, social and financial health
‘Dress for Your Day’ – enjoy the freedom to wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you.

When everyone is included, everyone wins:

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTQI+ community, and anyone else who wants to join our family.

Ready to apply? We’re ready to hear from you. Apply now.

Company: Sharp & Carter Technology, Projects & Digital Brisbane
Ad Text:
Data Engineering Specialist 
Brisbane | 6-Month Contract | Competitive Daily Rate
  
We are seeking 2 Data Engineering Specialists to design, build and optimise scalable data pipelines and architectures. You'll play a key role in enabling high-quality data insights, improving data reliability, and supporting a growing data-driven culture.
 
Working within a high-performing Digital, Technology & Information team, you'll collaborate closely with stakeholders to deliver innovative, cloud-based data solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop and maintain robust data pipelines and integrations
Build and optimise data models, dashboards and reporting solutions
Implement CI/CD pipelines to automate deployments and improve quality
Ensure strong data governance, quality and security practices
Support stakeholders in understanding and leveraging data effectively
Contribute to continuous improvement and adoption of emerging technologies
About You
You're an experienced Data Engineer who thrives in fast-paced environments and enjoys delivering scalable, high-quality data solutions.
 
You will bring:

Proven experience in data engineering, ETL, or data integration
Strong SQL skills and experience with modern data platforms
Experience with tools such as Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Factory or similar
Exposure to CI/CD practices and DevOps pipelines
Solid understanding of data modelling and cloud-based data ecosystems
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Highly regarded:

Experience with Power BI, DAX, PySpark
Exposure to Azure or AWS data services
Experience with Azure DevOps, Tableau or SQL Server technologies
Why Apply?

Work on meaningful projects that impact millions of Queenslanders
Immediate start with high-impact delivery focus
Modern tech stack and collaborative team environment
Competitive daily rate
