Job Title: Data Analyst - Data Migration and Reporting
Ad Text:
Catholic Care Broken Bay (CCDBB), delivers a wide range of Social Services through its workforce of ~1000 staff. With the need for Social Services in Australia ever increasing, CCDBB has embarked on an ambitious multi-year enterprise wide Transformation, to lay the foundations for future growth, improve Client Care, improve staff working conditions, improve the ability to manage Risk and Compliance, and also launch new Services. As a result, this Transformation is in need of a Data Analyst, with a focus on Data Migration and Reporting.

The successful candidate will be a critical enabler of the CCDBB’s success, as part of a highly committed Transformation and Technology Team, and will get an opportunity to work across all lines of business, across an extremely diverse group of Social Services Programs, with exposure to SLT members and Senior Management. Working in the Not For Profit sector, the successful candidate will also feel the deep meaning of knowing their work contributes to helping CCDBB’s Clients who need care.

About the role
The Data Analyst – Data Migration and Reporting, is responsible for ensuring high‑quality, reliable, and strategically valuable data within the organisation’s Salesforce environment. This role has a strong focus on data migration from legacy systems, data modelling, and the development of insightful dashboards and reports using both Salesforce and Power BI. Working closely with Project Managers, Program Managers, technical teams, and business stakeholders, the Data Analys – Data Migration and Reporting plays a critical role in supporting successful system implementation, organisational reporting, and evidence‑based decision‑making.
  
Key Responsibilities:

Data Migration & Data Quality
Lead and execute end‑to‑end data migration activities from legacy systems into Salesforce.
Map, transform, cleanse, and validate data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency.
Develop and maintain data dictionaries, migration templates, and data quality rules.
Conduct data audits and reconciliation to ensure migrated data meets business and compliance requirements.
Collaborate with technical teams to troubleshoot data issues and optimise migration processes.
Reporting, Dashboards & Data Visualisation
Design, build, and maintain Salesforce dashboards, reports, and analytics.
Develop Power BI dashboards that integrate Salesforce and other organisational datasets.
Translate complex datasets into clear, meaningful visual insights.
Ensure reporting outputs align with organisational KPIs and program outcomes.
Business Analysis & Stakeholder Engagement
Work with business units to understand data needs and reporting requirements.
Facilitate workshops, requirements gathering sessions, and data validation activities.
Provide subject matter expertise on Salesforce data structures and reporting capabilities.
Project & Program Management Alignment
Contribute to project planning, risk management, and milestone tracking.
Prepare documentation including data migration plans, test scripts, and progress reports.
Support Program Managers and Project Managers with data insights and impact assessments.
Ensure all data‑related activities align with project governance and quality standards.
Continuous Improvement & Data Governance
Identify opportunities to streamline data processes and improve data integrity.
Support the development of data governance frameworks and standards.
Provide training and guidance to staff on data literacy and reporting tools.
  
Key Capabilities & Skills:

Technical Skills

Proficiency in Salesforce (Reports & Dashboards, Data Loader, Data Import Wizard).
Demonstrated experience in data migration and data cleansing.
Advanced Power BI skills (data modelling, DAX, visualisation best practice).
Experience with ETL/ELT concepts and tools, consuming relational databases, and REST/SOAP API’s required.
Experience with Microsoft Fabric, or other Lakehouse platforms (Snowflake, Databricks) desired but not necessary.
Understanding of relational databases and data governance principles.
Analytical & Problem‑Solving Skills

Ability to interpret complex datasets and identify patterns, risks, and insights.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
Ability to troubleshoot data issues and propose practical solutions.
Project & Stakeholder Skills
Experience working within project or program management environments.
Ability to manage competing priorities and deliver to deadlines.
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical information.
Collaborative working style with the ability to influence stakeholders.
Qualifications & Experience

Tertiary qualification in Information Systems, Data Analytics, Business, or related field.
Experience in Salesforce data analysis, data migration, or CRM analytics.
Experience developing dashboards in Salesforce and Power BI.
Experience in structured project environments (Agile, PRINCE2, PMBOK).
Salesforce certifications (Administrator, Data Architect, Business Analyst) highly regarded.
Power BI certification desirable.
Experience in the Not For Profit sector desirable.
Key Outcomes for the Role

Successful migration of accurate, high‑quality data from legacy systems into Salesforce.
Reliable dashboards and reports that support decision‑making.
Strong alignment with project and program management disciplines.
Improved organisational data literacy and confidence in Salesforce.
Enhanced data governance, data quality, and reporting consistency.

About Us 
Automic Group brings together modern purpose-built technology with a dynamic and experienced team to tailor solutions for companies and funds to understand, engage and manage their investors. We provide a cloud-native single registry, employee share plan and board management platform, and professional services including company secretarial, ESG, accounting and finance, and investor and media relations. 

Today, over 1,400 ASX listed and unlisted clients trust Automic’s 250 employees to manage their registry, investor and board requirements – and Automic is ranked number one on the ASX by number of companies serviced, with 750 listed clients. In the past two financial years, Automic has won 50%of IPOs and 70% of listed companies who have switched registries. 

Our culture is innately entrepreneurial, comprising people who thrive in an atmosphere where ideas, actions and initiative are encouraged. Our core values, Customer Success, Ambition, Equality and Team are at the foundation of who we are and how we operate.

We are growing and are looking for an experienced Senior Technical Business Analyst to join our team in Sydney!


About the Role

This role requires an experienced BA who can independently own complex system changes end-to-end and who can work closely with business and technical stakeholders to deliver build-ready requirements as our tech grows.

Key Responsibilities include:
Operating in strict accordance with Automic’s Information Security Management System (ISMS), ensuring compliance with security, governance, and data protection standards.
Leading end-to-end business analysis activities across new features, enhancements, and strategic initiatives.
Collaborating with senior management, IT, Sales, Marketing, and Operations to gather, analyse, and prioritise business requirements.
Facilitating and driving requirements workshops, solution design sessions, and stakeholder discussions.
Working closely with developers and UX designers to refine and clarify requirements throughout the delivery lifecycle.
Translating high-value business priorities into clearly defined Minimum Viable Products (MVPs).
Supporting testing and validation activities to ensure delivered solutions meet business intent.
Identifying risks, dependencies, and impacts across systems and processes.
Continuously improving analysis practices, documentation standards, and tools to enhance productivity, quality, and security.

About you!

 

To be successful in this role, we are looking for someone with:
A bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business, Finance, or related discipline.
At least 8+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst in enterprise software environments working within an Agile delivery framework
Demonstrated experience delivering across full software development life cycles.
Proven hands-on technical capability from functional requirements gathering to user stories & high-quality acceptance criteria & mapping multisystem impact analysis
Able to work independently owning delivery outcomes, escalating risk when required
Proven experience of leading structured workshops with key stakeholders able to manage up effectively to get the right outcomes
A systems level thinker, understanding cross system impacts
Experience within financial services is highly desirable, particularly within share/unit registry, managed funds, fund admin, wealth/ market infrastructure or investor lifecycle platforms

You’ll be part of a team where operational excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement are genuinely valued. This is an opportunity to deepen your expertise, contribute to meaningful outcomes, and grow your career within a high-performing, purpose-led fintech environment.

Hybrid working
